title: GitHub Copilot: Code With AI Now

modules.title: Setup and First Contact: Getting Copilot Running in Minutes
modules.sub: Install, configure, and get Copilot live in your editor
modules.description: You walk through installing GitHub Copilot and connecting it to your coding environment without the usual setup headaches. By the end, you've seen your first AI-generated suggestion appear in real code — and you know what just happened and why.

modules.title: How to Talk to Copilot: Comments, Context, and Prompting in Code
modules.sub: Learn how your words shape the code Copilot writes
modules.description: You experiment with writing comments and prompts that actually guide Copilot toward useful output instead of generic guesses. You discover how the code around your cursor acts as context — and how to use that to your advantage.

modules.title: Your First Real Feature: Building Something That Works
modules.sub: Ship a working feature using Copilot as your coding partner
modules.description: You build a small but complete feature on a beginner project, leaning on Copilot to draft logic, fill in functions, and move faster than you could solo. You also practice reading and verifying the suggestions before accepting them, so you stay in control.

modules.title: Debugging With Copilot: When It Helps and When It Lies
modules.sub: Use Copilot to fix bugs — and spot when it misleads you
modules.description: You feed broken code to Copilot and see how it responds — sometimes helpfully, sometimes confidently wrong. You build a mental model for when to trust its fixes, when to question them, and how to use its suggestions as a starting point rather than a final answer.

modules.title: Going Further: Codex, the CLI, and What's Coming Next
modules.sub: Explore Copilot beyond the editor and into the future
modules.description: You get a quick look at how Copilot extends into the command line and how the underlying Codex model connects to the broader AI coding ecosystem. You leave with a clear sense of where this technology is heading and how to keep leveling up on your own.

synopsis: You don't need to already be a great coder to use GitHub Copilot — but you do need to know how to work with it so it doesn't send you in circles. This course gets you past the setup confusion and into actually shipping code with AI as your co-pilot.
rationale: Beginner coders are one of the highest-demand audiences for AI coding tools, yet most tutorials assume prior fluency, leaving early learners unable to leverage tools that could dramatically accelerate their growth.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can use GitHub Copilot to write, debug, and extend code on a real beginner project — without getting stuck or blindly trusting output you don't understand.
source_signals: OpenCode – Open source AI coding agent
Code Llama, a state-of-the-art large language model for coding
